newsSince: Incremental M&A/financing news, no AI-node operational catalyst. On June 11 Skyworks reported strong participation in the Qorvo notes exchange (~89-93% tendered), stripping restrictive covenants. China SAMR review entered Phase II and management is now hopeful the ~$22B Qorvo merger closes late 2026 vs early 2027 (>$500M synergies). RF/analog exposure to AI/CPO/HBM remains thin.

verdicts.rankRationale: AI-node bottleneck lens: SWKS has weak direct HBM/advanced-packaging/AI-networking relevance. June news is merger/financing flow, not AI-node evidence. Prior verdict already flagged for removal; stays at the bottom with mild negative drift.
verdicts.bull: Combined Skyworks-Qorvo creates a $22B RF/analog leader with >$500M synergies and possible 2026 close.
verdicts.neutral: Mobile-concentrated RF/analog franchise; AI/edge exposure is selective and indirect, far from the HBM/packaging bottleneck.
verdicts.bear: No AI-node relevance; merger integration risk and mobile cyclicality dominate the story for an AI-node ranking.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Qorvo merger close and synergy capture; any genuine AI/datacenter design win.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Antitrust delay/integration risk; persistent lack of AI-node relevance keeps it bottom-tier.

verdicts.rankRationale: Co-packaged-optics lens: essentially no datacenter optical/cable exposure. June Qorvo-financing progress is irrelevant to CPO. Prior case flagged removal on weak CPO/HBM/packaging/test relevance; mild negative drift, hold at bottom.
verdicts.bull: Scale from the Qorvo deal could fund adjacency moves over time.
verdicts.neutral: RF/analog with limited optical relevance; CPO thesis does not run through this name.
verdicts.bear: Cleaner CPO bottleneck names dominate; SWKS adds no co-packaged-optics alpha and carries mobile concentration risk.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: An unexpected datacenter optical/connectivity content win.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Zero CPO relevance; remains a removal candidate vs purer optics names.
